No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links Natural Language Processing to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links Natural Language Processing to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The course explicitly lists 'medical' (醫學) as a key application area for NLP, directly supporting health innovation and research (Target 3.b).

Quality Education.reason: As an advanced university course, it provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and imparts relevant technical skills in AI/NLP for employment (Target 4.4).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course is fundamentally about technological innovation in AI, focusing on 'cutting-edge approaches' and 'large language models (LLMs)' to enhance scientific research and technological capabilities (Target 9.5).

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links Natural Language Processing to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links Natural Language Processing to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links Natural Language Processing to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: While highly indirect, NLP skills in processing textual data could potentially support public access to information (Target 16.10), though not explicitly stated.

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course enhances access to and knowledge sharing of science and technology (Target 17.6) and promotes the use of enabling information and communications technology (Target 17.8) through its focus on cutting-edge NLP and LLMs.
